calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment Entered On 6/04/2014
Off Calendar May-03-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AKIKO HEARD's Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment Entered On 6/04/2014.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Akiko Heard's motion to vacate renewal of judgment is denied.

The judgment debtor has the burden of proving, by a preponderance of the evidence, that the debtor is entitled to relief under CCP 683.170 from renewal of the judgment. (Fidelity Creditor Serv., Inc. v. Browne (2001) 89 CA4th 195, 199, 106.) A judge may vacate the renewal on any ground that would be a defense to an action on the judgment, including the ground that the amount of the renewed judgment is incorrect. (CCP 683.170(a)).

Here, Defendant fails to raise any meritorious defense, such as providing a reason why the money is not owed to Plaintiff (e.g. identity theft or statute of limitations). Defendant also does not dispute the amount of the debt. Rather, Defendant argues that the clerk erred in entering the judgment because Plaintiff failed to present evidence to the court that the debt belonged to Defendant and additionally, under CCP 585(b) the case should have been subject to a court judgment. (Cmplnt., 4:24-27; 5:3-19.)

The court in HSBC Bank Nevada, N.A. v. Aguilar (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th Supp. 6, held no prove up was required for a common count open book account action because the complaint asserted the essential information. (Id. at 11-12.) Here, Plaintiff's complaint also provided the essential information for a common counts cause of action and no prove up was necessary. (See Cmplnt., Common Counts.) Thus, the result of the clerk's entry of default was that defendant effectively admitted all of the allegations of plaintiff's complaint.

Plaintiff failed to prove by a preponderance of the evidence that she is entitled to relief.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
